title: Clinical Auditor
description: Production-grade agentic clinical intelligence platform with multi-agent architecture for rehab data analysis. Automated return-to-sport readiness adjudication with phase-state inference.
longDescription: Architected a production-grade agentic clinical intelligence platform using Python, MongoDB, and Google VertexAI (Gemini). Designed a ReAct-based multi-agent architecture with deterministic orchestration where specialized agents perform layered reasoning over aggregated, chronologically normalized multi-source rehab data.
features: ReAct-based multi-agent architecture with deterministic orchestration (temperature=0)
Specialized agents: Evidence-Based Audit, Phase Intelligence, Biomechanics Analyzer
MongoDB-backed ETL aggregation pipeline with event deduplication
Metadata enrichment and cross-source timeline fusion (clinical + VALD time-series)
Dynamic phase-state inference and protocol gate validation
Regression signal detection and automated return-to-sport readiness adjudication
challenges: Designed layered reasoning system over multi-source rehab data
Implemented event deduplication and metadata enrichment pipeline
Built cross-source timeline fusion combining clinical and VALD time-series data
Created deterministic orchestration with temperature=0 for consistent results
impact: Real-time phase-state inference across patient timelines
Unified clinical and performance data in single platform
Reduced manual audit time through intelligent automation

title: Clinician Agent for Stance Health
description: RAG-powered agent that reduced form-filling time by 40% for physiotherapy workflows. Scaled to 200+ concurrent users across 50+ clinics.
longDescription: Developed an intelligent RAG-powered agent system for Stance Health that revolutionized physiotherapy workflows by automating form-filling processes. The system leverages advanced retrieval-augmented generation to understand clinical context and automatically populate patient documentation, reducing administrative burden on clinicians.
features: Real-time form auto-completion using RAG architecture
Redis-based caching for sub-second response times
Phoenix Tracer integration for comprehensive monitoring and debugging
Scalable Docker containerization supporting 200+ concurrent users
AWS infrastructure with auto-scaling capabilities
Context-aware suggestions based on patient history
challenges: Optimized RAG pipeline to handle 200+ concurrent users with consistent performance
Implemented Redis caching strategy reducing database queries by 60%
Designed fault-tolerant system with 99.9% uptime across 50+ clinics
Built comprehensive monitoring using Phoenix Tracer for real-time debugging
impact: 40% reduction in form-filling time for clinicians
Deployed across 50+ clinics serving 200+ concurrent users

title: MCP Server & Client
description: Medical knowledge retrieval system with 95% accuracy. Processes 5,000+ queries daily with 99% API uptime.
longDescription: Built a high-performance medical knowledge retrieval system using ChromaDB vector database and BGE embeddings. The system provides accurate medical information retrieval for healthcare professionals, processing thousands of queries daily with exceptional accuracy and reliability.
features: ChromaDB vector database for semantic search
BGE embeddings for medical domain understanding
Flask REST API with comprehensive documentation
LangChain integration for advanced query processing
Real-time query optimization and caching
Multi-tenant architecture for clinic isolation
challenges: Achieved 95% retrieval accuracy through fine-tuned BGE embeddings
Optimized ChromaDB indexing for sub-100ms query response times
Implemented robust error handling maintaining 99% API uptime
Designed scalable architecture handling 5,000+ daily queries

title: Exercise Monitoring System
description: Real-time pose estimation system that improved form accuracy by 35%. Processes 500+ video frames per second.
longDescription: Developed a cutting-edge computer vision system for real-time exercise monitoring and form correction. The system uses advanced pose estimation algorithms combined with DINO and SAM 2 for accurate body tracking and movement analysis, providing instant feedback to users and physiotherapists.
features: Real-time pose estimation with 30+ keypoint tracking
DINO-based object detection for equipment recognition
SAM 2 integration for precise body segmentation
Multi-angle video processing support
Automated form correction suggestions
Performance analytics and progress tracking
challenges: Optimized OpenCV pipeline to process 500+ FPS on standard hardware
Integrated DINO and SAM 2 models for enhanced accuracy
Developed real-time feedback system with <50ms latency
Implemented robust tracking across varying lighting conditions

title: Healthflex One-View
description: Real-time athlete analytics platform integrating VALD data. Reduced data latency by 50% for 100+ users.
longDescription: Created a comprehensive real-time analytics platform for athlete performance monitoring, integrating data from VALD performance testing equipment. The platform provides coaches and physiotherapists with instant insights into athlete metrics, injury risk assessment, and performance trends through interactive visualizations.
features: Real-time data streaming via WebSocket connections
FastAPI backend with async request handling
MongoDB for flexible athlete data storage
Interactive Plotly dashboards for data visualization
VALD equipment integration for performance metrics
Automated injury risk assessment algorithms
challenges: Implemented WebSocket architecture reducing data latency by 50%
Designed scalable MongoDB schema for 100+ concurrent users
Built real-time data pipeline processing 1000+ metrics per second
Created responsive Plotly visualizations for complex datasets
impact: 50% reduction in data latency for real-time insights
Adopted by 15+ sports teams and clinics

title: EEG-Controlled Prosthetic Arm
description: Brain-controlled prosthetic arm with 85% action accuracy. Patent pending for commercial development.
longDescription: Pioneered an innovative brain-computer interface system that enables users to control a prosthetic arm using EEG signals. The project combines machine learning, IoT hardware, and custom 3D-printed mechanical components to create an affordable and accessible prosthetic solution. Currently patent pending for commercial development.
features: EEG signal processing and classification using Scikit-Learn
Raspberry Pi-based control system for real-time processing
Custom 3D-printed mechanical arm design
Multi-gesture recognition (grasp, release, rotate)
Adaptive learning for user-specific signal patterns
Low-cost design (<$500 total component cost)
challenges: Achieved 85% action accuracy through advanced signal processing
Designed real-time ML pipeline on resource-constrained Raspberry Pi
Engineered custom 3D-printed components for durability and precision
Implemented adaptive learning system improving accuracy over time

title: Smart Streetlight Management
description: IoT solution for centralized streetlight control. Reduced maintenance response times by 20%. Smart India Hackathon 24' Semi-Finalist.
longDescription: Developed a comprehensive IoT-based smart streetlight management system for municipal infrastructure. The solution enables centralized monitoring and control of streetlights across large areas using LoRaWAN technology, significantly reducing energy consumption and maintenance costs. Recognized as a Semi-Finalist in Smart India Hackathon 2024.
features: LoRaWAN-based long-range communication network
Arduino-powered streetlight controllers
Real-time monitoring dashboard with Firebase backend
Automated fault detection and reporting
Adaptive brightness control based on ambient light
Energy consumption analytics and optimization
challenges: Implemented LoRaWAN network covering 5km+ radius
Designed low-power Arduino controllers with 2+ year battery life
Built scalable Firebase backend managing 500+ streetlight nodes
Reduced maintenance response times by 20% through automated alerts
